page: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes an Intel Core Processor (Broadwell) running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The shared CPU architecture results in poor single-core performance, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 800.09 and slow gzip compression speeds. However, multi-core execution is more robust, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6282.15 and a Passmark CPU score of 7986.55, both landing in the average tier. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 516.53 GB/sec, large-block write bandwidth is poor at 14.02 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced resource distribution, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance single-core execution. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis caching (achieving an average of 7.55 million operations per second), and moderate multi-threaded processing. It is less suitable for intensive database operations, heavy cryptography, or large language model inference due to weak performance in those specific benchmarks.
description: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a general-purpose Cloud Compute virtual server configured with 8 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6282.15 and a Passmark CPU score of 7986.55, though single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. This resource profile makes the server a cost-effective choice for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and Redis caching, where it processes an average of 7.55 million operations per second. It is less optimal for CPU-intensive single-threaded tasks or heavy database operations.
og_description: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a Cloud Compute server with 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 640 GB NVMe SSD. It offers balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining shared multi-core processing and local NVMe storage for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
640 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel tasks
Best fit for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose applications
